description: The chart encodes each country's value redundantly through both bar length (as expected) and bar thickness/height, so the perceived area grows roughly as the square of the value. This means the visual difference between Rwanda (~64%) and Cabo Verde (~44%) appears far more dramatic than the actual ~45% difference in percentages would suggest. A reader glancing at the chart will severely overestimate how much larger the top countries' representation is compared to the bottom of the list.
